text:
CT CHEST / ABDOMEN / PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

INDICATION: 64-year-old female with known metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma (right renal primary, resected), now with bilateral pulmonary metastases, right adrenal metastasis, and hepatic segment VI metastasis. Currently on cabozantinib 40 mg orally once daily (second-line therapy following progression on ipilimumab/nivolumab). This study is the second restaging scan since cabozantinib initiation, performed approximately 6 months from cabozantinib start. Comparison: prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed approximately 3 months ago (first restaging on cabozantinib).

TECHNIQUE: Helical CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was obtained following the administration of 100 mL of nonionic iodinated intravenous contrast (Omnipaque 350) with acquisition in the portal venous phase for the abdomen/pelvis and in the thoracic phase for the chest. Multiplanar reformations were generated in the coronal and sagittal planes. Images were reviewed on a PACS workstation with standard and lung windows.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The airways are patent to the segmental level bilaterally. No endobronchial lesion is identified. Mild bibasilar dependent atelectasis is present, unchanged.

Pulmonary Metastases: Four previously identified bilateral pulmonary metastases are again demonstrated, all exhibiting interval stability compared to the prior examination:

- Right upper lobe: A well-circumscribed, subpleural soft tissue nodule in the posterior segment of the right upper lobe measures 1.4 cm in greatest axial dimension (previously 1.5 cm on the prior scan, and 1.8 cm at the time of cabozantinib initiation). The lesion demonstrates smooth, lobulated margins with no surrounding ground-glass halo. Its appearance is stable. No cavitation.

- Right lower lobe: In the posterior basal segment of the right lower lobe, there is a heterogeneous nodule with irregular margins measuring 1.3 cm (previously 1.4 cm). This lesion was previously treated with stereotactic body radiotherapy (SBRT; 54 Gy in 3 fractions). The internal architecture remains somewhat heterogeneous with a small central focus of hypoattenuation, likely representing post-treatment fibrosis or treatment-related necrosis, consistent with local treatment effect. No convincing evidence of local recurrence at this site; the lesion appears to have reached a stable post-treatment configuration.

- Left lower lobe: A round, smoothly marginated nodule in the superior segment of the left lower lobe measures 1.1 cm in axial diameter (previously 1.2 cm, and 1.4 cm at the time of cabozantinib initiation). Stable in morphology and size.

- Left upper lobe: A small, rounded nodule in the lingula of the left upper lobe measures 0.7 cm (previously 0.7 cm). Essentially unchanged.

No new pulmonary nodules are identified above the threshold of reliable detection. No consolidation, cavitary lesion, or pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. No evidence of treatment-related pneumonitis.

Mediastinum and Hilum: The mediastinal and hilar contours are unremarkable. No pathologic mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. The heart is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of dissection or aneurysm.

Bones (Chest): No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions are identified in the imaged thoracic skeleton. Degenerative changes are noted in the thoracic spine, including mild anterior osteophyte formation at multiple levels. No suspicious periosteal reaction.

Soft Tissues (Chest): Unremarkable.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver and Biliary System: The liver is normal in size with smooth hepatic contours. A previously identified lesion in hepatic segment VI (posterior right lobe) is again identified as a round, hypoattenuating focus measuring 1.2 cm in axial dimension (previously 1.2 cm; 1.6 cm at the time of cabozantinib initiation). The lesion demonstrates peripheral rim enhancement with a persistently hypoattenuating center, morphologically consistent with a metastatic deposit. Its size is stable compared to the prior study and represents sustained reduction from the pre-cabozantinib baseline. No additional hepatic lesions are identified to suggest new hepatic metastatic seeding. No biliary ductal dilatation. The gallbladder is surgically absent (noted: no prior cholecystectomy documented; gallbladder is present but unremarkable with no stones). The common bile duct is of normal caliber at 4 mm.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas: The pancreas is normal in size and morphology with homogeneous parenchymal attenuation. The main pancreatic duct is not dilated. No peripancreatic stranding or fluid collection.

Adrenal Glands: The left adrenal gland is unremarkable in size and appearance. The right adrenal gland demonstrates a 1.2 cm nodule in the medial limb (previously 1.3 cm; 1.5 cm at the time of cabozantinib initiation and prior to SBRT consideration). The nodule is mildly hypervascular on portal venous phase imaging with delayed washout characteristics that remain indeterminate for lipid content on this contrast-enhanced series alone, but is consistent with the known biopsy-confirmed metastatic deposit from clear cell renal cell carcinoma. The lesion appears marginally smaller than on the prior examination and has not enlarged. No evidence of adrenal hemorrhage.

Kidneys: The right kidney has been surgically resected (radical nephrectomy). The right retroperitoneum demonstrates expected post-surgical changes: fat stranding and soft tissue fibrosis in the renal fossa, without discrete nodularity or mass formation to suggest local recurrence. The left kidney is normal in size and enhancement pattern, without hydronephrosis or filling defects in the collecting system. No perinephric abnormality. Mild cortical thinning of the left kidney is noted, consistent with physiologic compensatory hypertrophy given the solitary kidney status.

Retroperitoneum / Lymph Nodes: The retroperitoneal lymph node chains are within normal size limits. No discrete pathologic lymphadenopathy identified. This remains stable from the prior examination. The aorta and inferior vena cava are normal in caliber. The IVC shows no evidence of intraluminal thrombus at the prior thrombectomy site. Small volume retroperitoneal soft tissue stranding is present consistent with post-surgical change, unchanged.

Bowel: The small bowel and large bowel loops appear normal in caliber and wall thickness. No obstruction, free air, or perforation. No mesenteric lymphadenopathy.

Peritoneum / Ascites: No peritoneal nodularity or ascites.

---

PELVIS:

Bladder: The urinary bladder is partially distended with smooth wall contour. No intraluminal filling defects or mucosal irregularity.

Gynecologic Organs: The uterus is small and atrophic, appropriate for the patient's age. No adnexal masses. No pelvic free fluid.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No pelvic lymphadenopathy. The iliac chain lymph nodes are within normal size limits bilaterally.

Bones (Pelvis): Bilateral iliac crests, sacrum, and proximal femora are without suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions. There are mild degenerative changes at the right hip, with narrowing of the superolateral joint space and small subchondral cysts, consistent with moderate osteoarthritis. No fracture.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Continued stable metastatic burden on cabozantinib therapy at the six-month mark from initiation, representing sustained disease control across all measurable sites when compared to both the immediate pre-cabozantinib baseline and the prior restaging scan. No new sites of metastatic disease.

2. Bilateral pulmonary metastases (right upper lobe 1.4 cm, right lower lobe 1.3 cm post-SBRT, left lower lobe 1.1 cm, left upper lobe 0.7 cm) -- all stable to marginally smaller. The right lower lobe lesion continues to show post-treatment morphology consistent with local SBRT effect and no convincing signs of in-field regrowth.

3. Hepatic segment VI metastasis: 1.2 cm, stable compared to prior examination, representing a sustained decrease from baseline size of 1.6 cm.

4. Right adrenal metastasis (biopsy-confirmed): 1.2 cm, marginally smaller than the preceding scan (1.3 cm), and reduced from 1.5 cm at the time of cabozantinib initiation.

5. Post-right radical nephrectomy changes in the right renal fossa without evidence of local recurrence.

6. No new lymphadenopathy, hepatic lesions, osseous metastases, or peritoneal disease.

7. Mild right hip osteoarthritic changes, as noted on prior bone scan correlation; no osseous metastatic lesion at this site.

Reviewing Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Abdominal Imaging / Oncologic Imaging Division]
Report status: FINAL
